novel_features.name: Upload and wait for indexing
novel_features.description: Upload a local video file or register a public URL, then poll Twelve Labs until indexing reaches a terminal status.
novel_features.rationale: Editors need one reliable command that gets a long video into an index before downstream analysis begins.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Use this before analysis when you need the CLI to wait until the video is usable.

novel_features.name: Editor-ready video brief
novel_features.description: Generate a deterministic JSON or Markdown editing plan with title, topics, hashtags, chapters, highlights, and recommended cuts.
novel_features.rationale: The raw API returns analysis primitives; this command shapes them into an output an editor can use directly.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Use this when you want to avoid manually watching a long video just to find the strongest moments.

novel_features.name: Video embedding workflow
novel_features.description: Create a video embedding task from a local file or public URL and optionally wait for ready results.
novel_features.rationale: A simplified embedding workflow hides the raw endpoint split and polling mechanics for semantic video workflows.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Use this when you need embeddings for deeper search, matching, or semantic workflows.

novel_features.name: Local clip cutting from plans
novel_features.description: Cut local clip files with ffmpeg from a video-brief JSON plan without inventing timestamps.
novel_features.rationale: The API finds the moments; this command turns those ranges into files for downstream editing.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Use this after video-brief when you have the source video locally and want clip files immediately.

narrative.headline: Turn long videos into editor-ready cut plans.
narrative.value_prop: This CLI wraps the Twelve Labs API and adds workflow commands for video upload, indexing waits, structured briefs, embeddings, and local clip cutting. Agents can go from a long source video to JSON or Markdown editing guidance without manually watching the whole file.
